details: lib/transport.js imports both fs and http/https and, at line 262, walks the filesystem via fs.readdirSync as part of a module whose declared purpose is network transport. The combination — directory enumeration composed with outbound HTTP/HTTPS in a single transport module — matches the credential/file exfiltration shape rather than any legitimate transport function. Stage-1 tracing engaged the file and made tool calls, but its output was withheld by the provider's malware-output safety filter — a corroborating signal that the traced code reads as operational exfiltration content. Installing or loading this package permits reading of files from the installer's host and shipping their contents to the network destinations wired into transport.js.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
